default_rule.description: Default stress falls on the final syllable of the word

exception_rules.description: The negative copula 'deyil' is stressed on the first syllable: DEY-il
Question particles (mi/mı/mu/mü) do not receive stress; stress remains on the preceding word
Negation suffix -ma/-mə shifts stress to the syllable before it
Proper names in vocative form retain stress on the root (first) syllable
Recent loanwords (Russian, English) may retain original stress patterns
Heç (none/never) and similar intensifiers stress first syllable
Postpositions (üçün, ilə, kimi, etc.) are unstressed

phrasal_stress.description: Rules for stress within a phrase (prosodic domain)

noun_phrase.description: In a noun phrase, the adjective modifier is more prominent than the noun

verb_phrase.description: The finite verb receives the main phrasal stress

focus_stress.description: Focused element (new information) receives the highest pitch accent

syllabification_rules.description: Rules for dividing words into syllables (required for stress placement)
syllabification_rules.comment: Each syllable has exactly one vowel nucleus; consonant clusters prefer onset position

stress_notation.description: How stress is marked in phoneme output
